creator: emaste@freebsd.org
text:
Indeed, this appears to be reproducible with racing non-root invocations of install.

% sh -c "while true; do install -m 555 foo bar; done" &
% sh -c "while true; do install -m 555 foo bar; done" &

install: bar: Permission denied
install: bar: Permission denied
install: bar: Permission denied
install: bar: No such file or directory
...

No errors are reported when those are run as root.


Looking at the METALOG from my 8 core system (which hasn't reproduced the issue) shows:

feynman% grep -c './usr/tests/bin/pkill/pkill-i_test '/tank/emaste/obj/root/freebsd/METALOG
29

i.e., this (and other pkill tests) are installed 29 times.

Of 1571 duplicated installs I see in METALOG, 1546 are in usr/tests
